rationale: Metastatic ccRCC upregulates CXCR4-driven homing while losing renal tubule identity marker PAX8, so their difference should widen in M1 versus M0 tumors.
Metastatic ccRCC tumors upregulate invasive MMP9 while losing differentiated proximal tubule marker LRP2, so their log-difference should separate M1 from M0.
Metastatic ccRCC upregulates the invasion protease MMP9 while losing differentiated proximal tubule marker LRP2, so their log-difference should rise in M1 tumors.
SPP1 up-regulation drives invasion/metastasis while CA9 is typically high in localized ccRCC and can decrease in aggressive dedifferentiated tumors, so their difference should elevate in M1 cases.
S100A4 upregulation drives EMT and metastatic dissemination while CALB1 loss reflects dedifferentiation of renal tubule identity, so their difference should amplify in M1 tumors.
Elevated basement-membrane remodeling via COL4A2 alongside loss of differentiated tubular marker SLC12A1 reflects invasive dedifferentiation characteristic of metastatic ccRCC.
High CXCR4 drives metastatic homing while preserved PAX8 marks differentiated renal epithelium, so their contrast should separate M1 from M0 tumors.

High VEGFA reflects HIF-driven angiogenic switch that supports metastatic dissemination, while loss of the tubular transporter SLC22A8 marks dedifferentiation, so their difference should widen in M1 versus M0 tumors.

MMP9 drives invasive/metastatic phenotypes while LDHB reflects more oxidative, differentiated tumor metabolism that tends to be retained in indolent M0 ccRCC, so their difference should elevate in M1.

SPP1 (osteopontin) is upregulated in metastatic ccRCC and promotes invasion, while LRP2 marks preserved proximal tubule identity that is lost in aggressive disease, so their difference should widen in M1 tumors.
S100A4 upregulation drives EMT and metastatic dissemination while CUBN marks preserved proximal tubule differentiation that is lost in aggressive metastatic ccRCC.
COL4A2 reflects basement-membrane remodeling during metastatic invasion while CALB1 marks preserved distal-tubule identity lost in aggressive ccRCC, so their difference should track M1 vs M0.
High CXCR4 drives metastatic homing while loss of the renal tubular differentiation marker PAX8 accompanies dedifferentiation, so their difference should rise in M1 versus M0.
Elevated VEGFA-driven angiogenesis together with loss of normal tubular epithelial identity (SLC12A1) marks aggressive, metastasis-prone ccRCC.
MMP9 drives invasive metastatic behavior while NDUFA4L2 marks the HIF-adapted indolent ccRCC phenotype, so their difference should rise in M1 versus M0 tumors.
SPP1 (osteopontin) is a secreted pro-metastatic factor elevated in aggressive ccRCC, while LRP2/megalin reflects preserved proximal tubular differentiation that is lost with metastatic progression.
Metastatic ccRCC upregulates glycolytic PDK1 while losing differentiated tubular AGXT expression, so this difference should widen in M1 tumors.
S100A4 upregulation drives EMT and metastatic dissemination while CALB1 marks preserved distal tubule differentiation, so their contrast should widen in M1 versus M0 tumors.

CXCR4 drives metastatic homing and is upregulated in M1 disease, while PAX8 marks differentiated renal epithelium retained in localized M0 tumors, so their difference should separate metastatic from non-metastatic KIRC.
COL4A2 upregulation reflects basement-membrane remodeling in invasive tumors while SLC12A1 loss marks dedifferentiation of renal tubule identity, so their difference should rise in M1 disease.
High MMP9 drives invasive metastatic behavior while NDUFA4L2 marks the indolent hypoxia-adapted ccRCC state, so their difference should rise in M1 versus M0.
SPP1 is upregulated in invasive/metastatic ccRCC while LRP2 marks differentiated proximal tubule epithelium lost in aggressive disease, so their contrast should separate M1 from M0.
High VEGFA driven by HIF activation promotes angiogenic metastatic spread while loss of albumin-expressing tubular differentiation reflects aggressive dedifferentiated ccRCC.
S100A4 drives EMT and metastatic dissemination while CALB1 marks preserved distal tubule identity lost in aggressive ccRCC, so their difference should widen in M1 tumors.
CXCR4 drives metastatic dissemination while PAX8 reflects retained renal tubular differentiation, so the contrast should rise in M1 versus M0 tumors.
COL4A2 upregulation reflects basement-membrane remodeling in metastatic ccRCC while AGXT loss marks dedifferentiation of proximal tubule identity, so their difference should widen in M1 tumors.
MMP9-driven ECM remodeling is elevated in metastatic KIRC while loss of differentiated tubular SLC12A1 expression reflects dedifferentiation, so their difference should separate M1 from M0.

SPP1 is strongly upregulated in metastatic ccRCC driving invasion, while LDHB (favoring oxidative metabolism) is typically retained in less aggressive tumors, so their difference should separate M1 from M0.
Hypoxia-driven VEGFA rises with metastatic angiogenic switch while loss of differentiated tubule marker LRP2 reflects dedifferentiation in M1 ccRCC.
Elevated S100A4 drives invasive EMT programs while loss of the distal-tubule marker CALB1 reflects dedifferentiation, so their difference should rise in M1 metastatic ccRCC.

SPP1 is upregulated in metastatic renal cell carcinoma promoting invasion and immune evasion, while PTGER3 marks differentiated renal tubule identity that is lost upon metastatic progression, so a high SPP1−PTGER3 score should distinguish M1 from M0 tumors.
Metastatic ccRCC upregulates the pro-invasive secreted phosphoprotein SPP1 while losing renal tubule identity marker CUBN, so their difference should be markedly higher in M1 than M0 tumors.

SPP1 (osteopontin) is upregulated in metastatic ccRCC promoting invasion and immune evasion, while CALB1 (calbindin) marks differentiated distal tubule identity that is progressively lost during malignant progression, so their difference amplifies the metastatic signal.

Metastatic ccRCC shows elevated proliferative activity (MKI67) alongside loss of renal tubule transporter identity (SLC22A8), so their difference is expected to be markedly higher in M1 versus M0 tumors.

HIF2α (EPAS1) is the master driver of ccRCC and is further elevated in metastatic disease, while AGXT marks differentiated proximal tubule identity that is progressively lost during metastatic dedifferentiation, so their difference should be strongly positive in M1 versus M0 tumors.

CXCR4 drives homing of tumor cells to distant organs and is upregulated in metastatic ccRCC, while LDHB (pyruvate-producing direction of LDH) tends to be suppressed in highly glycolytic, dedifferentiated tumors, so their difference should be large and positive in M1 versus M0 cases.

Metastatic ccRCC upregulates aerobic glycolysis (LDHA) while losing renal tubular differentiation markers (SLC12A1, a thick-ascending-limb transporter), so their difference should be markedly more positive in M1 than M0 tumors.

In metastatic ccRCC, hypoxia-driven CA9 expression escalates disproportionately relative to the glycolytic enzyme ENO2, so a high CA9 − ENO2 score reflects extreme HIF activation beyond the Warburg shift and should enrich for M1 tumors.

SPP1 (osteopontin) is a well-established pro-metastatic secreted protein upregulated in aggressive RCC, while PTGER3 marks differentiated renal tubular identity that is lost upon dedifferentiation and metastasis, so their difference should be strongly positive in M1 versus M0 tumors.

Metastatic ccRCC tumors exhibit elevated proliferative activity (high TOP2A) alongside loss of renal tubular differentiation markers (low CALB1), so their difference should be strongly positive in M1 versus M0 cases.

In metastatic ccRCC, HIF-driven VEGFA is strongly upregulated while the renal lineage transcription factor PAX8 is progressively lost during dedifferentiation, so a high VEGFA − PAX8 difference should discriminate M1 from M0 tumors.

CXCR4 is transcriptionally driven by HIF and promotes hematogenous dissemination in ccRCC, while LDHB is epigenetically silenced in renal cancer and further suppressed as tumors dedifferentiate, so the difference CXCR4 − LDHB is expected to be markedly higher in M1 than M0 tumors.

In metastatic ccRCC, Warburg-driven LDHA is upregulated to fuel invasion while the proximal-tubule organic-anion transporter SLC22A8 is progressively silenced, so their difference should increase monotonically from M0 to M1.

COL4A2, a stromal extracellular matrix component upregulated during EMT-driven invasion, increases in M1 tumors while SLC12A3, a renal tubule transporter reflecting differentiated epithelial identity, is progressively silenced, making their difference a sensitive index of dedifferentiation-driven metastatic progression in clear-cell RCC.

S100A4 promotes cytoskeletal remodeling and invasive migration while LRP2 (megalin) is a hallmark of differentiated renal proximal tubule identity that is lost upon dedifferentiation, so their difference is expected to be markedly higher in metastatic (M1) than organ-confined (M0) clear-cell RCC.

Metastatic ccRCC tumors upregulate the proliferation marker MKI67 while losing the proximal-tubule enzyme AGXT, so a high MKI67 − AGXT score reflects the combined de-differentiation and aggressive growth that drives M1 disease.

HIF-2α (EPAS1) is constitutively activated in ccRCC and further elevated in metastatic tumors, while CALB1 marks differentiated distal tubule epithelium that is progressively lost with dedifferentiation, making their difference a strong separator of M0 from M1 disease.

SPP1 (osteopontin) is a secreted phosphoprotein strongly upregulated in metastatic ccRCC that promotes tumor cell invasion and immune evasion, so its expression relative to the housekeeping baseline of ACTB should be markedly elevated in M1 versus M0 tumors.
